seeds.title: Erdos q(6): minimum edges in a 6-uniform intersecting hypergraph with cover number 6
seeds.query: q(6) minimum edges 6-uniform pairwise-intersecting hypergraph cover number six exact witness verifier 5-cover rejection orderly generation canonical augmentation tau-deficiency prune bracket 14 18
seeds.falsifier: A proposed witness has two disjoint edges or admits a hitting set of size at most 5; an optimality claim lacks a complete independently checkable nonexistence certificate.
seeds.verifier: Given an edge list of 6-sets, check every edge pair intersects and exhaustively reject every hitting set of size at most 5. Any edge gives cover number at most 6, so no 5-cover certifies cover number exactly 6. Optimality still requires a complete nonexistence certificate for every smaller edge count.
seeds.next_action: Replay the exact witness verifier after its final write and record its SHA-256, with known-good and known-bad fixtures whose assertions check the intended rejection reason, not only the final valid/invalid boolean. Then execute one bounded orderly-generation/canonical-augmentation step with a tau-deficiency prune; do not repeat the failed SAT/DRAT encoding and do not claim optimality without complete exhaustion.
seeds.avoid: Do not repeat the failed SAT/DRAT route: D(5,12) and D(6,14) already timed out with global tau-constraint explosion. Do not launch broad search or claim q(6); use orderly generation/canonical augmentation with tau-deficiency pruning only after exact verifier and kill fixtures. No Atlas writes or external submissions.

seeds.title: Erdos #1029 finite frontier: diagonal Ramsey R(5,5)
seeds.query: diagonal Ramsey R(5,5) bracket 43 46 exact K5-free graph complement verifier 42 vertex Exoo witness 43 vertex extension 656 critical graphs SAT DRAT orderly generation
seeds.falsifier: A lower-bound witness contains a 5-clique in the graph or its complement; an upper-bound claim lacks a complete independently checkable nonexistence certificate.
seeds.verifier: Given a graph, enumerate 5-cliques in it and its complement. Absence in both is an exact lower-bound witness; optimality or an upper-bound improvement requires complete certified exhaustion.
seeds.next_action: Replay the exact graph/complement K5 verifier and the known 42-vertex witness, then execute one bounded extension or perturbation cell that is not already covered by the 656 known critical K42 graphs. Content-address every final witness or negative shard.
seeds.avoid: Do not infer the asymptotic Erdos #1029 conjecture from a finite R(5,5) result. Do not repeat the exhausted direct extension farm without a changed structural condition, and do not claim an upper bound from solver timeout. No Atlas writes or external submissions.

seeds.title: Erdos #552: settle the n=17 C4-versus-star Ramsey cell at m=22
seeds.query: R(C4,S17) m22 simple graph C4-free minimum degree at least 5 exact codegree verifier SAT DRAT Parsons upper bound certificate
seeds.falsifier: A proposed 22-vertex red graph has a vertex pair with codegree at least 2 or minimum degree below 5; a nonexistence result has no replayable DRAT or LRAT certificate.
seeds.verifier: For m=22 and n=17, require every vertex-pair codegree at most 1 and every red degree at least m-n=5. These exact checks certify a witness; UNSAT requires a replayable proof certificate.
seeds.next_action: Replay the checked-in Erdos-552 verifier/certificate package, assert known-good and branch-specific known-bad fixtures, then run one bounded n=17,m=22 construction or certified SAT shard.
seeds.avoid: Do not conflate a solver timeout with nonexistence, and do not re-prove the closed n=12..16 cells. A 22-vertex witness implies R(C4,S17) >= 23. The displayed 21-vertex Hermitian-polarity fixture has degree range 4..5 and 48 edges, so it is not the m=22 witness and not SRG(21,5,0,1). Do not call an edge addition C4-safe from the endpoints' existing common-neighbor count alone; replay the full pair-codegree invariant and branch-specific kill fixtures. Random no-hit output is not route inaccessibility or extension impossibility. Keep witness and UNSAT directions distinct. No Atlas writes or external submissions.

seeds.title: Erdos #140 finite frontier: r_3(212)
seeds.query: r_3(212) 3-term arithmetic-progression-free subset 43 known witness search for 44 exact verifier two hard nonexistence chunks DRAT LRAT
seeds.falsifier: A proposed set has fewer than the claimed elements or contains a nontrivial 3-term arithmetic progression; an exact upper bound lacks complete certified exhaustion of the remaining hard chunks.
seeds.verifier: Given A subset [212], verify cardinality and reject every distinct a<b<c with a+c=2b. A 44-set improves the lower bound; exactness requires a complete nonexistence certificate.
seeds.next_action: Replay the exact 3-AP verifier on known-good and rejection-reason fixtures, then execute one bounded structurally targeted search cell for a 44-set or one explicitly manifested hard nonexistence shard.
seeds.avoid: Do not report another 43-set as a frontier improvement and do not claim r_3(212)=43 from incomplete CP-SAT, MIP, or CDCL runs. No Atlas writes or external submissions.

seeds.title: Erdos #1 finite frontier: distinct-subset-sum a(11)
seeds.query: distinct subset sums a(11) bracket 310 594 size 11 Conway Guy exact 2048 subset-sum verifier incremental collision backtracking local search
seeds.falsifier: A proposed 11-set has duplicate subset sums or a largest element not below the standing upper bound; a lower-bound claim lacks a complete deterministic shard manifest and replay transcript.
seeds.verifier: Enumerate all 2048 subset sums of the 11 positive integers and require pairwise distinctness. This certifies only an upper-bound witness; optimality needs complete exhaustive-search evidence.
seeds.next_action: Replay a tiny exact subset-sum verifier with collision-reason fixtures and the Conway-Guy control, then execute one bounded canonical-prefix backtracking or local-search cell below 594.
seeds.avoid: Do not confuse the finite a(11) record with the $500 asymptotic headline and do not claim optimality from a witness or partial search. No Atlas writes or external submissions.

seeds.title: Erdos #41 finite frontier: shortest 11-mark B3 ruler
seeds.query: shortest B3 ruler 11 marks a(11) upper bound 445 distinct multiset triple sums exact verifier backtracking SAT DRAT
seeds.falsifier: A proposed 11-mark ruler has two equal multiset triple sums or length at least 445; an exact-value claim lacks a complete independently replayable nonexistence certificate below the witness.
seeds.verifier: Enumerate all 286 nondecreasing-index triple sums for 11 marks and require pairwise distinctness. A shorter ruler is only an upper-bound improvement; exactness needs certified nonexistence.
seeds.next_action: Build and replay the multiset-triple-sum verifier with fixtures that distinguish repeated-index triples, then run one bounded canonical backtracking cell seeking length below 445.
seeds.avoid: Do not substitute ordinary three-distinct-element sums for multiset B3 sums, and do not infer the open asymptotic liminf conjecture from this finite record. No Atlas writes or external submissions.

seeds.title: Erdos #67 finite frontier: unrestricted discrepancy C=3 beyond 130000
seeds.query: Erdos discrepancy unrestricted C=3 length 130000 plus minus one sequence homogeneous arithmetic progression exact verifier multiplicative restricted distinction
seeds.falsifier: A proposed sequence has a homogeneous arithmetic progression whose running signed sum has absolute value above 3; a claim based only on multiplicative sequences does not address the unrestricted frontier.
seeds.verifier: For every step d, scan the running prefix sums of x_d,x_2d,... and require absolute value at most 3. This exact O(n log n) check certifies an unrestricted witness when no multiplicativity assumption is imposed.
seeds.next_action: Replay the unrestricted discrepancy verifier on branch-specific fixtures and the standing 130000 control, then execute one bounded extension or structured repair cell and reverify from scratch.
seeds.avoid: Do not conflate the exact multiplicative value 127645 with the open unrestricted C=3 frontier, and do not treat the asymptotic theorem as a finite record. No Atlas writes or external submissions.

seeds.title: Erdos #86 finite frontier: C4-free subgraph of Q7 above 304 edges
seeds.query: ex(Q7,C4) hypercube Q7 C4-free subgraph more than 304 edges exact 672 square verifier simulated annealing structured local search
seeds.falsifier: A proposed edge set is not a subgraph of Q7, has at most 304 edges, or contains all four edges of a hypercube 4-cycle; an exact-value claim lacks certified exhaustion.
seeds.verifier: Validate every edge has Hamming distance 1 and scan the 672 fixed 4-cycles of Q7, rejecting any fully present square. A witness above 304 improves only the lower bound.
seeds.next_action: Replay the exact Q7 edge/square verifier with invalid-edge and hidden-square fixtures, then run one bounded symmetry-aware local-search cell seeded from the 304-edge construction.
seeds.avoid: Do not mix Q7 and Q8 records, do not claim the asymptotic density conjecture, and do not infer exactness from failure to beat 304. No Atlas writes or external submissions.
